fit_assessment.rationale: Genstar Capital scores HIGH on all three axes. Sector fit is unambiguous: the industrials portfolio — Arrowhead Engineered Products (380,000+ SKUs, 23 global facilities, active aftermarket parts sourcing), OTC Industrial Technologies (60+ locations, industrial distribution), Tekni-Plex (specialty materials/packaging), MW Industries (precision components manufacturing), and TERREPOWER (remanufactured auto parts) — constitutes a dense cluster of procurement-intensive businesses with real supply chains, global sourcing exposure, and high COGS sensitivity. Deal activity is active: Arrowhead is executing a live buy-and-build with multiple recent add-ons (Red Hawk, Custom Golf Car Supply), Fund XI ($12.6B, 2023) is actively deploying, and Genstar just formalized its supply chain value creation capability on February 24, 2026 with the appointment of John Kufner to the Strategic Advisory Board. Access is exceptional: Kufner (LinkedIn confirmed at linkedin.com/in/john-kufner) is a named, reachable individual whose prior role was literally 'MD, Procurement & Supply Chain' at Bain Capital — he ran PE portfolio procurement programs and will immediately grasp the value proposition without needing to be educated.
fit_assessment.why_now: John Kufner was appointed to Genstar's Strategic Advisory Board on February 24, 2026 — within days of this assessment — with an explicit mandate to lead supply chain initiatives across the industrials portfolio. He is actively standing up this function right now, which means he is in the process of scoping exactly what external procurement support he needs, assessing portfolio company supplier bases, and identifying where outside expertise can accelerate value creation. This is the window before his program is fully resourced and the decisions about external partners are locked in.
fit_assessment.key_hook: John Kufner's appointment on February 24, 2026 as Genstar's SAB Member for Industrials & Supply Chain — his mandate directly mirrors procurement consulting scope, and his Bain Capital MD of Procurement & Supply Chain background makes him the ideal champion who already speaks this language.
objections: We already have John Kufner on the SAB handling supply chain — we don't need outside procurement help. Counter: Kufner's role is strategic and portfolio-wide; he will need execution-level procurement consulting support for specific company transformations, supplier rationalization projects, and DD work on new acquisitions — exactly the kind of project-based engagement SoloSail.ai delivers. His Bain background means he's already experienced in deploying outside procurement specialists alongside internal PE value creation resources.

Arrowhead and our other portfolio companies have their own procurement teams. Counter: At 380,000+ SKUs across 23 facilities with active add-on integrations (Red Hawk, Custom Golf Car Supply), Arrowhead's procurement complexity exceeds what any internal team can optimize alone. Each add-on acquisition brings new supplier relationships, duplicate vendor contracts, and rationalization opportunities that benefit from a specialist with cross-industry benchmarking data and no internal political constraints.

We use management consulting firms for this kind of work. Counter: Large consulting firms deploy junior teams at 3–5x the day rate, with months-long engagements that extend beyond the integration window. A specialized procurement consultant offers senior-practitioner expertise, faster mobilization, and project-based pricing — a format PE firms increasingly prefer for discrete value creation sprints between close and exit.
fit_assessment.urgency_signal: Kufner's appointment is less than a week old as of this assessment. He is actively defining his mandate, identifying portfolio priorities, and determining what external resources he needs — the program is being designed right now. Reaching him before his external partnerships are established and his first wave of portfolio projects is resourced is a time-limited opportunity that closes as his function matures.
fit_assessment.recommended_outreach_angle: Open by acknowledging his appointment — briefly, specifically, and without flattery. Reference that you work with PE-backed industrials companies on procurement value creation and that Genstar's portfolio (name Arrowhead specifically, citing the 380K SKU / add-on complexity) represents exactly the kind of work you specialize in. Lean into his Bain Capital background: he already understands that external procurement specialists complement internal PE operational resources, because he was the one deploying them at Bain. The pitch isn't 'here's what procurement consulting is' — it's 'here's how I work alongside SAB members like yourself to execute on the supply chain agenda you're now building.' Frame it as a capability augmentation conversation, not a sales call.

pitch_package.email_subject: Genstar supply chain mandate — Arrowhead integration scope
pitch_package.email_body:
Four days into your new role on Genstar's Strategic Advisory Board, you're already looking at a portfolio that would test any supply chain program: Arrowhead Engineered Products alone is managing 380,000+ SKUs across 23 facilities while simultaneously integrating Red Hawk and Custom Golf Car Supply — two distinct add-ons, each with its own supplier base, vendor contracts, and category overlap that needs to be rationalized before the combined cost structure compounds further. That's before you get to OTC Industrial Technologies, where 60+ locations and 40 service shops represent a vendor contract standardization and spend analytics opportunity that most multi-site industrial distributors at that scale haven't fully harvested. Genstar's buy-and-build cadence means these integration mandates don't arrive one at a time — and the procurement DD and post-close rationalization work on each new add-on has to be ready to mobilize the week a deal closes.

I run a specialized procurement consulting practice focused exclusively on PE-backed manufacturing and industrial distribution companies — the kind of project-based, senior-practitioner support that complements a PE firm's internal value creation function rather than duplicating it. At a prior industrial distribution platform, I mapped and consolidated a fragmented vendor base across 18 locations, reducing active supplier count by 34% and recovering 210 basis points of COGS margin within six months of close. At a multi-site discrete manufacturer, I completed procurement due diligence on three sequential add-ons for the same PE sponsor, delivering category-level spend benchmarks and integration risk flags prior to each closing — work that was directly used in purchase price negotiations. These aren't illustrative examples; they're the same profile as Arrowhead and OTC, which is exactly why I'm writing.

Given that you're currently defining which external resources will support your supply chain program at Genstar, I'd welcome a 20-minute conversation to walk through how I've worked alongside SAB-level operators at other PE sponsors — specifically how I've structured procurement DD and integration sprints so they fit within a deal team's timeline rather than extending it. If it's useful, I can share a brief case note from a comparable aftermarket-parts integration before we talk.
pitch_package.brief:
**Why this firm:** Genstar Capital's industrials portfolio is one of the most procurement-intensive in the mid-market PE universe. Arrowhead Engineered Products — a 2021 platform investment — operates 380,000+ SKUs across 23 global facilities and is actively executing a buy-and-build strategy including the Red Hawk and Custom Golf Car Supply add-ons, each generating a distinct supplier rationalization mandate. OTC Industrial Technologies, a 2019 platform, runs 60+ locations and 40 service shops serving 13,000+ customers — a scale at which vendor contract standardization and spend analytics represent direct EBITDA leakage. Genstar's explicit buy-and-build thesis across the industrials vertical means procurement due diligence and post-close integration support are recurring, predictable needs — not one-off engagements.

**Why this contact:** John Kufner was appointed to Genstar's Strategic Advisory Board on February 24, 2026 with an explicit mandate to lead supply chain initiatives across the industrials portfolio. His prior role — Managing Director, Procurement & Supply Chain at Bain Capital — means he ran PE procurement value creation programs and was the buyer of external procurement consulting support. He will immediately understand the value proposition and has no need for a conceptual pitch about why procurement matters in PE. Additional PE operating experience as COO at Platinum Equity–backed Cook and Boardman Group and Blackstone-backed Noho Commerce reinforces that he evaluates external resources from an operator's perspective.

**Why now:** Kufner's appointment is four days old. He is actively scoping what external procurement resources his program requires — before partners are selected and his first wave of portfolio projects is staffed. This window closes as his function matures.
talking_points: Walk me through how you're thinking about sequencing the procurement work across Arrowhead's recent add-ons — with Red Hawk and Custom Golf Car Supply each bringing their own vendor relationships into the platform, are you planning to run supplier rationalization at the Arrowhead platform level, or letting each acquired entity hold its own category structure through an initial integration period?

At OTC Industrial Technologies — 60-plus locations, 40 service shops — the vendor contract fragmentation across that many sites typically shows up as a spend analytics problem before it shows up as a negotiation opportunity. What does OTC's current visibility into cross-location spend look like, and is that something you're planning to address in the near term?

From your time at Bain Capital running the procurement and supply chain function, you deployed external procurement specialists alongside your internal value creation resources on portfolio company work — I'm curious how you're thinking about that same model here at Genstar: what does the external-versus-internal split look like for the supply chain program you're building?

Genstar's buy-and-build cadence in industrials means procurement DD on add-ons is a recurring need rather than a one-time event. How far in advance of a close is your team typically able to bring in functional specialists for category-level spend benchmarking and supplier concentration risk — or is that more often a post-close exercise given deal timelines?

With six active industrial platforms — Arrowhead, OTC, TERREPOWER, MW Industries, Tekni-Plex, Sonny's — each at a different stage of the hold period and buy-and-build program, where do you see the highest-urgency procurement work sitting right now as you're coming into this mandate?
